message:
test(robot): add SQLite, adapter, and restart-recovery integration tests [bd-j9jo3.9.2]

Add 11 integration tests covering realistic operational sequences:
- Source outage/recovery with degraded feature detection
- Multiple source flapping with state transitions
- Attention feed cursor continuity and replay
- Subscription delivery verification
- Incident promotion from repeated evidence and critical types
- Duplicate event suppression via dedup map
- Cooldown resurfacing after window expiration
- Operator acknowledgment persistence
- Operator snooze state and expiry
- Concurrent feed access with cursor monotonicity

Fixes test assumptions to match actual implementation:
- AppendDeduplicated returns (event, published) not (event, suppressed)
- First event must use AppendDeduplicated to populate dedup map
- Replay returns (events, newestCursor) where newestCursor is feed max
- Source names must match featureDeps map ("caut" not "quota")
- CollectedAt must be updated for all sources in each phase
- RFC3339 snooze comparison needs second-level padding

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.5 <noreply@anthropic.com>

 {"id":"bd-j9jo3.8.3","title":"robot-redesign/parity: align SSE and WebSocket behavior with durable attention semantics","description":"Align server-side streaming transports with the same durable attention semantics used elsewhere. This task exists because once attention and digest become restart-safe operator primitives, streaming transports must stop inventing their own event meanings.","design":"The work should ensure SSE and WebSocket streams honor the same cursor, replay, resync, degraded-state, request-correlation, idempotency, aggregation, cooldown, resurfacing, operator-attention-state, action-handoff, and recoverable-error semantics as CLI and REST consumers. It should also decide how stream initialization, backlog replay, and handoff to live updates behave when items are pinned, snoozed, acknowledged, muted, or manually escalated. This task is about semantic alignment, not merely plumbing bytes over a socket.","acceptance_criteria":"This task is complete when streaming transports can be treated as alternative delivery mechanisms for the same durable, deduplicated, operator-shaped, and request-correlated event product rather than as bespoke feeds.","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","estimated_minutes":180,"created_at":"2026-03-21T23:38:25.318193079Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-03-26T22:04:11.598201887Z","closed_at":"2026-03-26T22:04:11.597568532Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["parity","robot-redesign","streaming"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-j9jo3.8.3","depends_on_id":"bd-j9jo3.1.10","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-03-22T00:23:34.759084751Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-j9jo3.8.3","depends_on_id":"bd-j9jo3.1.11","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-03-22T00:29:51.524008109Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-j9jo3.8.3","depends_on_id":"bd-j9jo3.1.12","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-03-22T00:48:00.515239538Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-j9jo3.8.3","depends_on_id":"bd-j9jo3.1.7","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-03-22T00:02:37.977760883Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-j9jo3.8.3","depends_on_id":"bd-j9jo3.1.9","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-03-22T00:17:53.634972268Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-j9jo3.8.3","depends_on_id":"bd-j9jo3.3.5","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-03-22T01:36:35.335495935Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-j9jo3.8.3","depends_on_id":"bd-j9jo3.4.4","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-03-21T23:40:13.032379944Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-j9jo3.8.3","depends_on_id":"bd-j9jo3.4.6","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-03-22T00:23:44.380223071Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-j9jo3.8.3","depends_on_id":"bd-j9jo3.4.7","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-03-22T00:30:03.926006101Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-j9jo3.8.3","depends_on_id":"bd-j9jo3.4.8","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-03-22T00:48:08.573117873Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-j9jo3.8.3","depends_on_id":"bd-j9jo3.8","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-03-21T23:38:25.318193079Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-j9jo3.8.3","depends_on_id":"bd-j9jo3.8.1","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-03-21T23:38:36.107092492Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}],"comments":[{"id":338,"issue_id":"bd-j9jo3.8.3","author":"ubuntu","text":"Watch-semantics follow-up: SSE and WebSocket parity now explicitly covers heartbeats, filtered subscriptions, replay-to-live handoff, liveness deadlines, backpressure or boundedness markers, and consistent meaning for quiet periods.","created_at":"2026-03-22T00:49:04Z"},{"id":355,"issue_id":"bd-j9jo3.8.3","author":"ubuntu","text":"Sensitivity follow-up: SSE and WebSocket parity must prove that streaming and replay handoff preserve the same disclosure-control behavior as CLI and REST, including no-leak guarantees for safe previews and redaction markers.","created_at":"2026-03-22T01:37:24Z"},{"id":377,"issue_id":"bd-j9jo3.8.3","author":"codex","text":"Priority raised to P1 after plan-space audit: streaming parity is a user-facing contract and a disclosure boundary, not secondary plumbing. Once 4.8 and 8.1 are done, SSE and WebSocket should converge before validation or docs treat the redesign as finished.","created_at":"2026-03-26T21:09:25Z"}]}
 {"id":"bd-j9jo3.9","title":"robot-redesign/validation: tests, docs, transition, and legacy cleanup","description":"Add the deterministic fixtures, unit tests, schema goldens, integration coverage, Go-based E2E drivers, benchmark regressions, documentation refresh, transition notes, and legacy-path removal needed to make the redesign durable instead of aspirational. This epic exists because contract-heavy work only becomes real when behavior is pinned down, debugging is practical, cost regressions are visible, and the old assembly paths are retired safely.","design":"The redesign will touch contracts, SQLite persistence, event replay, explainability, renderers, and multiple transports. That means we need: checked-in example payloads and scenario matrices; shared deterministic Go test harnesses and fault injectors under testdata; package-local _test.go unit suites; integration coverage for persistence, degraded recovery, restart safety, and diagnostic evidence flow; Go-based E2E drivers under e2e/ that exercise CLI, REST, and streaming parity with verbose logging of commands, cursors, schema IDs, source_health, incidents, explainability handles, and assertion diffs; benchmark and regression coverage for payload size, latency, diagnostics cost, and storage churn; and explicit cleanup of superseded paths.

This epic is where we force the new architecture to become the only architecture, rather than one more parallel layer.","acceptance_criteria":"This epic is complete when deterministic Go-based unit, integration, benchmark, and E2E coverage pin the redesign's contract, ergonomics, explainability, and cost profile with great detailed logging; repository docs are self-contained and point to the example corpus and test entry points; and legacy robot assembly paths have been removed so the shared projection architecture is the only supported path.","notes":"Do not declare success early just because the new snapshot output looks good once. The bar is parity, restart safety, contract stability, comprehensive automated verification, and removal of redundant logic. Validation beads are part of the architecture, not optional QA garnish.","status":"open","priority":4,"issue_type":"epic","estimated_minutes":300,"created_at":"2026-03-21T23:32:13.892254319Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-03-22T00:12:54.073288880Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["robot-redesign","validation"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-j9jo3.9","depends_on_id":"bd-j9jo3","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-03-21T23:32:13.892254319Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}],"comments":[{"id":321,"issue_id":"bd-j9jo3.9","author":"ubuntu","text":"Future self: the cleanup work is part of the feature, not optional janitorial follow-up.","created_at":"2026-03-21T23:32:14Z"}]}
 {"id":"bd-j9jo3.9.1","title":"robot-redesign/validation: add schema goldens and contract stability tests","description":"Add golden tests and contract stability checks for robot payload schemas, checked-in example payloads, and registry-backed discovery output. This task exists because the redesign only improves long-term quality if contract drift becomes obvious and explainable.","design":"The tests should pin checked-in schema artifacts, normative example payloads and scenario transcripts, registry-generated capabilities/help/schema views, representative payload shapes for core surfaces, canonical error or remediation examples for invalid cursor, resync-required, bounded-result, duplicate-request, and invalid-inspect conditions, request identity or idempotency examples, correlated actuation outcomes, operator-attention-state examples such as acknowledge or snooze or pin transitions, and diagnostic explainability examples such as explanation-handle shapes, evidence summaries, suppression markers, and why-this-is-stale or truncated flows. They should make breaking changes explicit, keep additive changes disciplined, and log schema IDs, example names, changed sections, error codes, request or outcome IDs, operator-state markers, diagnostic handles, and high-signal diffs when goldens move. The focus is not on testing every field serializer in isolation; it is on safeguarding the declared contract and its concrete examples.","acceptance_criteria":"This task is complete when schema, example, and discovery drift trigger obvious test failures with rich diagnostics, and maintainers can evolve the robot contract only through explicit intentional updates to schemas and examples, including retry-safe, attention-hygiene, and operator-override semantics.","notes":"Use the example corpus as an executable contract, not as a bag of arbitrary snapshots.","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","estimated_minutes":180,"created_at":"2026-03-21T23:38:26.297471362Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-03-26T21:43:01.378318402Z","closed_at":"2026-03-26T21:43:01.377959981Z","close_reason":"Implemented schema goldens and contract stability tests:

Golden files created:
- 9 schema goldens (status, snapshot, attention, digest, 5 inspect surfaces)
- Registry surfaces golden (135 surfaces)
- 7 canonical error response goldens

Contract stability tests added:
- TestGolden_CoreSchemas, TestGolden_Registry, TestGolden_ErrorResponses, TestGolden_ScenarioPayloads
- TestContract_RequiredSchemaFields, TestContract_ErrorCodeStability
- TestContract_RegistrySurfaceStability, TestContract_IdempotencySupport

Usage: Run with -update-goldens to regenerate when contracts change.

Commit: reated_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-j9jo3.9.1","depends_on_id":"bd-j9jo3.6.3","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-03-21T23:40:15.315308706Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-j9jo3.9.1","depends_on_id":"bd-j9jo3.6.4","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-03-21T23:40:15.839232826Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-j9jo3.9.1","depends_on_id":"bd-j9jo3.6.6","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-03-21T23:48:16.937186344Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-j9jo3.9.1","depends_on_id":"bd-j9jo3.6.7","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-03-22T00:10:37.649785822Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-j9jo3.9.1","depends_on_id":"bd-j9jo3.7.2","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-03-21T23:40:13.617275560Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-j9jo3.9.1","depends_on_id":"bd-j9jo3.7.3","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-03-26T19:44:58.855397443Z","created_by":"codex","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-j9jo3.9.1","depends_on_id":"bd-j9jo3.9","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-03-21T23:38:26.297471362Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-j9jo3.9.1","depends_on_id":"bd-j9jo3.9.6","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-03-21T23:48:16.306571964Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}]}
-{"id":"bd-j9jo3.9.10","title":"robot-redesign/validation: add benchmark and regression coverage for latency, payload size, and storage churn","description":"Add automated benchmark and regression coverage for the user-facing cost profile of the redesign. This task exists because robot mode ergonomics depend not only on semantic correctness but also on snapshot, status, digest, attention, inspect, and transport behavior staying fast, bounded, and operationally cheap as projects grow.","design":"The coverage should use the shared deterministic fixture corpus and Go testing infrastructure to measure snapshot, status, digest, attention, inspect, diagnostics, and renderer behavior under small, medium, and large scenarios plus repeated-event, retry-heavy, and operator-override-heavy scenarios. It should track payload bytes, section counts, raw event counts versus surfaced item counts where observable, truncation or pagination activation, latency, allocations, rows touched or rewritten where observable, replay throughput, diagnostic expansion cost, deduplication effectiveness, operator-state impact on surfaced queue size, and any storage-churn signals needed to verify SQLite stays operationally cheap. Use Go benchmarks and targeted regression tests rather than ad hoc shell loops. The logging should record scenario IDs, schema IDs, request IDs where relevant, render format, item counts, payload sizes, operator-state markers, suppression or resurfacing markers, diagnostic-surface depth where relevant, and enough supporting detail that a regression report explains what got slower or larger and why that matters to users.","acceptance_criteria":"This task is complete when the redesign has automated benchmark and regression coverage for latency, payload size, storage churn, compact-attention effectiveness, and operator-control overhead, with detailed logs that make regressions obvious and diagnosable. The result should prove that boundedness and performance budgets are being honored for representative scenarios rather than merely hoped for.","notes":"Fast, bounded outputs are part of the contract for agent-facing robot mode. Benchmark work should protect user experience and operational cost, not chase meaningless micro-optimizations.","status":"open","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","estimated_minutes":210,"created_at":"2026-03-21T23:55:45.991262114Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-03-26T19:45:40.379226712Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["benchmark","performance","robot-redesign","validation"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-j9jo3.9.10","depends_on_id":"bd-j9jo3.1.11","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-03-22T00:29:57.571500782Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-j9jo3.9.10","depends_on_id":"bd-j9jo3.1.6","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-03-21T23:57:28.813873196Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-j9jo3.9.10","depends_on_id":"bd-j9jo3.2.4","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-03-21T23:57:29.449135271Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-j9jo3.9.10","depends_on_id":"bd-j9jo3.3.5","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-03-22T01:36:39.832167367Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-j9jo3.9.10","depends_on_id":"bd-j9jo3.4.6","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-03-22T00:23:48.140664593Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-j9jo3.9.10","depends_on_id":"bd-j9jo3.4.7","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-03-22T00:30:09.314787309Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-j9jo3.9.10","depends_on_id":"bd-j9jo3.4.8","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-03-22T00:48:12.943189537Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-j9jo3.9.10","depends_on_id":"bd-j9jo3.5.4","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-03-22T01:26:11.351998992Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-j9jo3.9.10","depends_on_id":"bd-j9jo3.6.1","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-03-21T23:57:30.103760616Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-j9jo3.9.10","depends_on_id":"bd-j9jo3.6.2","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-03-21T23:57:30.760403187Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-j9jo3.9.10","depends_on_id":"bd-j9jo3.6.3","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-03-21T23:57:31.431902328Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-j9jo3.9.10","depends_on_id":"bd-j9jo3.6.4","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-03-21T23:57:32.090277353Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-j9jo3.9.10","depends_on_id":"bd-j9jo3.6.5","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-03-21T23:57:32.790945414Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-j9jo3.9.10","depends_on_id":"bd-j9jo3.6.6","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-03-21T23:57:33.501646942Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-j9jo3.9.10","depends_on_id":"bd-j9jo3.6.7","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-03-22T00:10:40.688435708Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-j9jo3.9.10","depends_on_id":"bd-j9jo3.8.1","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-03-21T23:57:34.326567098Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-j9jo3.9.10","depends_on_id":"bd-j9jo3.8.2","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-03-26T19:45:12.368939372Z","created_by":"codex","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-j9jo3.9.10","depends_on_id":"bd-j9jo3.8.3","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-03-21T23:57:35.045374334Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-j9jo3.9.10","depends_on_id":"bd-j9jo3.9","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-03-21T23:55:45.991262114Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-j9jo3.9.10","depends_on_id":"bd-j9jo3.9.6","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-03-21T23:57:35.744902360Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}],"comments":[{"id":341,"issue_id":"bd-j9jo3.9.10","author":"ubuntu","text":"Watch-semantics follow-up: benchmark and regression coverage should measure idle-heartbeat overhead, filter selectivity costs, cadence adaptation under churn and degraded sources, and the operational cost difference between durable event publication and ephemeral keepalive traffic.","created_at":"2026-03-22T00:49:06Z"},{"id":345,"issue_id":"bd-j9jo3.9.10","author":"ubuntu","text":"Historical-performance follow-up: benchmarks must measure replay and as-of latency, cache-hit versus reconstruction cost, payload-size behavior for historical responses, and SQLite churn introduced by replay caching so the feature stays operationally cheap enough for live debugging.","created_at":"2026-03-22T01:28:15Z"},{"id":361,"issue_id":"bd-j9jo3.9.10","author":"ubuntu","text":"Sensitivity follow-up: benchmark coverage must measure redaction and safe-preview cost on hot surfaces and replay paths so the safety layer remains cheap enough for routine robot consumption.","created_at":"2026-03-22T01:37:28Z"},{"id":370,"issue_id":"bd-j9jo3.9.10","author":"codex","text":"Plan-space refinement: benchmark coverage now explicitly waits for 8.2 so user-facing cost checks include dashboard and alternate-renderer projection work, not only CLI renderers. Measure payload, allocation, and redaction or safe-preview overhead where those projections surface compact evidence.","created_at":"2026-03-26T19:45:40Z"}]}

 {"id":"bd-j9jo3.9.11","title":"robot-redesign/validation: add redaction, sensitivity, and disclosure-control verification coverage","description":"Add dedicated verification for sensitivity, redaction, and disclosure-control semantics so ntm can prove richer robot data stay safe across persistence, replay, diagnostics, and transport boundaries.","design":"The tests should cover contract examples for visible versus preview_only versus redacted versus withheld payloads; rule-version and reason markers; safe-preview generation for pane output, mail snippets, diagnostic evidence, and replay snapshots; no-leak guarantees in SQLite projections, attention journals, incident replay caches, REST payloads, and streaming events; allowlisted-field behavior; and explicit diagnostic explanations for why content was transformed. Coverage should include package-local unit tests, SQLite and restart integration tests, Go-based e2e scenarios, and benchmark or regression checks for redaction cost and payload-size effects. Logging should be excellent: scenario name, schema ID, rule version, disclosure state, hidden-field counts, hashed evidence handles, replay mode, and diffs that prove raw secrets were not emitted.","acceptance_criteria":"This task is complete when the redesign has explicit automated coverage proving that sensitive material is transformed or withheld consistently across live surfaces, replay, persistence, diagnostics, and parity transports, and when failures produce enough detailed logging that a maintainer can understand exactly what leaked, what was expected, and which rule or surface misbehaved.","notes":"Sensitivity handling is too important to leave as an implied side effect of generic tests. If a regression could leak a secret without a dedicated failing test and clear artifact trail, this task is incomplete.","status":"open","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","estimated_minutes":210,"created_at":"2026-03-22T01:34:08.838581790Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-03-26T21:14:46.082586209Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["robot-redesign","sensitivity","validation"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-j9jo3.9.11","depends_on_id":"bd-j9jo3.1.14","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-03-22T01:36:40.673125135Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-j9jo3.9.11","depends_on_id":"bd-j9jo3.1.5","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-03-22T01:36:41.531532027Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-j9jo3.9.11","depends_on_id":"bd-j9jo3.3.5","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-03-22T01:36:42.403226219Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-j9jo3.9.11","depends_on_id":"bd-j9jo3.5.4","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-03-22T01:36:43.306247021Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-j9jo3.9.11","depends_on_id":"bd-j9jo3.6.7","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-03-22T01:36:44.270220056Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-j9jo3.9.11","depends_on_id":"bd-j9jo3.8.1","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-03-26T21:07:55.154188545Z","created_by":"codex","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-j9jo3.9.11","depends_on_id":"bd-j9jo3.8.2","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-03-26T19:45:16.279676807Z","created_by":"codex","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-j9jo3.9.11","depends_on_id":"bd-j9jo3.8.3","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-03-22T01:36:45.281933126Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-j9jo3.9.11","depends_on_id":"bd-j9jo3.9","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-03-22T01:34:08.838581790Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-j9jo3.9.11","depends_on_id":"bd-j9jo3.9.1","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026- If a failure can erase or obscure operator trust history without a focused failing test, this task is incomplete.","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","estimated_minutes":210,"created_at":"2026-03-22T01:56:56.309450111Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-03-22T16:43:09.985831755Z","closed_at":"2026-03-22T16:43:09.985369821Z","close_reason":"Verified implemented. audit_validation_test.go (494 lines): TestAuditEventStructure, ActorIDFormats, AttentionAcknowledge, AttentionSnooze, ActuationSend, ActuationInterrupt, IncidentCreate/Escalate/Resolve, DisclosureRedact, QueryByActor, QueryByTargetRef, RetentionPolicy, RedactionDoesNotObscureActor. audit_attribution_test.go (778 lines): ActorAttributionOnAttentionStateChange, ActuationRequestOutcomeTrail, IncidentLifecycleHistory, VisibleInDiagnostics, SensitivityRedactionPreservesUsefulness, MultiActorSessionMutation, PersistenceAcrossRestart.  {"id":"bd-j9jo3.9.2","title":"robot-redesign/validation: add SQLite, adapter, degraded-source, and restart-recovery integration tests","description":"Add integration coverage for the runtime projection store, adapter normalization, degraded-source handling, attention replay, watermark persistence, incident evolution, and restart recovery behavior. This task exists because the hardest parts of the redesign live at the seams between subsystems, not inside isolated helper functions.","design":"The tests should exercise realistic sequences such as source outage followed by partial recovery, ntm restart followed by resumed delta computation, incident promotion after repeated evidence, projection rebuild with durable cursor continuity, ambiguous timeout followed by safe retry, duplicate request suppression with later verified outcome, repeated flapping source with cooldown and resurfacing, acknowledgment followed by material change and resurfacing, snooze expiry, pin and manual escalation behavior, retention or pruning decisions, and partial transport resync. They should use the shared deterministic harness and log scenario IDs, request IDs, idempotency keys, cursor transitions, watermark state, source_health changes, operator-state transitions, suppression or resurfacing markers, incident fingerprints, and assertion diffs with enough detail to diagnose failures quickly. This task should prove the redesign works as an operational system, not just as a collection of structs.","acceptance_criteria":"This task is complete when the critical persistence, degradation,  {"id":"bd-pqwk","title":"Support partial/prefix session names for disambiguation","description":"Support partial/prefix session names for disambiguation.

## Problem Being Solved

Currently you need to type full session name like `vnt destructive_command_guard`. Should support prefix matching.

## Behavior

- If prefix matches exactly one session: use it
- If prefix matches multiple sessions: show matching options, ask user to be more specific
- If prefix matches zero sessions: show error with available sessions

## Examples

```bash
# Currently required
ntm view destructive_command_guard

# Desired
ntm view destr  # works if only one session starts with 'destr'
vnt destr       # same with alias

# Ambiguous
ntm view my
# Error: 'my' matches multiple sessions: my_project, my_test
# Please be more specific.
```

## Implementation

Update session name resolution in all commands:
- ntm view SESSION
- ntm kill SESSION
- ntm send SESSION
- ntm health SESSION
- etc.

## Robot Mode API
Robot mode requires exact names (no fuzzy matching).

## Acceptance Criteria

1. Prefix matching works for all commands taking SESSION arg
2. Exact match preferred over prefix match
3. Multiple matches show list and error
4. Zero matches show available sessions
5. Robot mode requires exact names
6. Works with vnt alias

## Unit Tests (80% coverage target)
- internal/session/resolve_test.go
- Test exact match priority
- Test unique prefix resolution
- Test ambiguous prefix error

## E2E Test Script
```bash
#!/bin/bash
# [E2E-PREFIX] Session Prefix Resolution Tests
LOG_PREFIX="[E2E-PREFIX]"
echo "$LOG_PREFIX Testing prefix session names"
ntm spawn test_session_one --cc=1 --no-prompt
ntm spawn test_session_two --cc=1 --no-prompt
ntm view test_session_o && echo "Prefix worked"
ntm kill test_session_one --force
ntm kill test_session_two --force
echo "$LOG_PREFIX Prefix test complete"
```","acceptance_criteria":"1. Partial session names work for disambiguation
2. Prefix matching finds unique session
3. Multiple matches returns error with suggestions
4. Works with all session-accepting commands
5. Unit tests for matching logic
6. E2E test with multiple sessions","notes":"## Testing Requirements
- Unit tests: core logic, error paths, edge cases.
- E2E scripts: full CLI/robot flow with detailed log/slog prefixes (e.g., [E2E-<AREA>]) and include session/agent/bead IDs.
- If structured outputs are affected, assert schema stability + deterministic ordering.
","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"feature","created_at":"2026-01-19T06:46:15.575351270Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-02-01T23:00:17.536990467Z","closed_at":"2026-02-01T23:00:17.536922981Z","close_reason":"Prefix session-name resolution (exact>prefix), wired across core CLI commands; robot/json requires exact; unit tests","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0}
 {"id":"bd-ptu2","title":"Task: Add event markers and details overlay","description":"## Overview
Add discrete event markers on the timeline with hover/select details.

## Deliverables
1. Event marker types:
   - Prompt sent (▶): When user sends prompt to agent
   - Completion (✓): When agent finishes a task
   - Error (✗): When error detected
   - Start/Stop (◆): Session lifecycle events

2. Marker rendering:
   - Small icons above timeline bar at event time
   - Different colors for different event types
   - Clustered markers when events too close together

3. Details overlay:
   - Show popup when marker selected
   - Include: timestamp, event type, details
   - For prompts: first 50 chars of message
   - For errors: error message

4. Interaction:
   - Arrow keys to navigate between markers
   - Enter to expand marker details
   - Esc to close overlay

## Implementation Notes
- Use unicode symbols for markers
- Popup positioning to avoid edge overflow
- Cache marker positions for efficient navigation

## Acceptance Criteria
- Markers appear at correct times
- Different event types visually distinct
- Details overlay shows on selection
- Navigation between markers works
- Unit tests for marker placement","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-01-19T18:28:03.496917374Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-01-22T04:20:48.617338102Z","closed_at":"2026-01-22T04:20:48.617205812Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-ptu2","depends_on_id":"bd-3h0n","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-19T18:28:50.481921380Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-ptu2","depends_on_id":"bd-f815","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-01-19T18:28:03.512975659Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}]}
 {"id":"bd-puov1","title":"Task: Write comprehensive unit tests for Synthesis system","description":"Unit tests for output collection, merging, synthesis, auditing, and formatting.

## Test Coverage

### OutputCollector (collector_test.go)
- Collect single/multiple
- Partial outputs
- Validation + filtering
- ToSynthesisInput

### OutputCapture (capture_test.go)
- CapturePane happy path (mock tmux)
- Section parsing
- Token estimation
- MaxLines truncation

### Mechanical Merging (merge_test.go)
- Group findings by evidence
- Deduplicate recommendations
- Detect contradictions

### Synthesizer + Templates (synthesizer_test.go)

 {"id":"bd-q8kii","title":"Task: Web UI Safety deck - redaction/privacy controls","description":"## Scope
Extend the Safety deck to include:
- redaction mode selector (off|warn|redact|block)
- privacy mode toggle / session indicator
- safety profile selector (if implemented)

## Notes
- UI must call canonical REST endpoints (do not embed logic client-side).
- UI must never display raw secrets; only redacted placeholders.

## Tests
- E2E: change redaction mode → verify REST + WS update.

## Acceptance Criteria
- User can view and update redaction mode from the Safety deck.
- User can view privacy-mode state and understand what is disabled.
- UI never renders raw sensitive substrings; only placeholders.
- E2E test covers a mode change end-to-end.","status":"closed","priority":3,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-02-01T03:35:16.703135470Z","created_by":"OrangeWolf","updated_at":"2026-02-11T17:52:39.021786946Z","closed_at":"2026-02-11T17:52:39.021768291Z","close_reason":"Web UI Safety deck implemented: internal/privacy/ for redaction/privacy controls, internal/redaction/ for data redaction.","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-q8kii","depends_on_id":"bd-20xv1","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-01T06:08:44Z","created_by":"import","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-q8kii","depends_on_id":"bd-ldo6o","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-02-01T06:08:44Z","created_by":"import","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}]}

 {"id":"bd-qjsu","title":"E2E Tests: File reservation with real Agent Mail","description":"## E2E Test: ntm + Agent Mail File Reservation Integration

**Note:** File reservation is implemented in MCP Agent Mail. This tests ntm's integration with that existing system.

### Prerequisites
- MCP Agent Mail server running (`am` health check passes)
- Test workspace with beads

### Test Scenarios

1. **Basic reservation flow**
   - Spawn agent via ntm
   - Assign bead with file paths
   - Verify Agent Mail shows reservation for agent
   - Complete bead
   - Verify reservation released

2. **Conflict detection**
   - Spawn 2 agents
   - Agent 1 assigned bead touching foo.go
   - Agent 2 assigned bead touching foo.go
   - Verify ntm reports conflict from Agent Mail
   - Verify holder info shown

3. **Reservation on assign**
   - Run ntm assign with --auto
   - Verify file_reservation_paths called for each assignment
   - Check Agent Mail state reflects reservations

### Verification Commands
```bash
# Check Agent Mail for reservations
# (use Agent Mail MCP tools or am CLI)
```

### Logging
- Log Agent Mail API calls
- Log reservation state at each step
- Log conflict details

## Test File
